nodes.title: Buck Mulligan
nodes.snippet: Stately, plump / Buck Mulligan / I paid the rent.

nodes.title: Stairhead
nodes.snippet: came from the stairhead / mounted the round gunrest / parapet

nodes.title: Mirror and a razor
nodes.snippet: a mirror and a razor lay crossed / He fears the lancet of my art as I fear that of his.

nodes.title: Tower
nodes.snippet: tower / his post / Elsinore. That beetles o'er his base into the sea

nodes.title: In Troy
nodes.snippet: equine in its length / grained and hued like pale oak / a cloud of coalsmoke and fumes of fried grease

nodes.title: Genuine Christine
nodes.snippet: For this, O dearly beloved, is the genuine Christine: body and soul and blood and ouns.

nodes.title: Those white corpuscles
nodes.snippet: A little trouble about those white corpuscles / Switch off the current

nodes.title: Plump shadowed face
nodes.snippet: The plump / shadowed face and sullen oval jowl recalled a prelate, patron of arts in the middle ages.

nodes.title: Absurd name
nodes.snippet: Your absurd name, an ancient Greek!

nodes.title: Malachi
nodes.snippet: Malachi Mulligan, two dactyls / to flash the tidings abroad / Mercurial Malachi

nodes.title: Greeks!
nodes.snippet: We must go to Athens. Will you come / Ah, Dedalus, the Greeks! I must teach you. You must / read them in the

nodes.title: Black panther
nodes.snippet: He was raving all night about a black panther, Stephen said. Where is his guncase?

nodes.title: Paresis
nodes.snippet: lunatic / General paralysis of the insane!

nodes.title: Snotgreen
nodes.snippet: A new art colour for our Irish poets: snotgreen.

nodes.title: Dublin Bay
nodes.snippet: Dublin bay / ring of bay and skyline

nodes.title: Great sweet mother
nodes.snippet: what Algy calls it: a great sweet mother

nodes.title: Kingstown pier
nodes.snippet: harbourmouth of Kingstown / on the pier

nodes.title: Repent!
nodes.snippet: you killed your mother / begging you with her last breath to kneel down and pray for her. And you refused. / Silent with awe and pity I went to her bedside.

nodes.title: Wax and rosewood
nodes.snippet: Silently, in a dream she had come to him after her death, her wasted body within its loose brown graveclothes giving off an odour of wax and rosewood, her breath, that had bent upon him, mute, reproachful, a faint odour of wetted ashes. / In a dream, silently, she had come to him, her wasted body within its loose graveclothes giving off an odour of wax and rosewood, her breath, bent over him with mute secret words, a faint odour of wetted ashes.

nodes.title: His hand
nodes.snippet: the wellfed voice / a voice that speaks to her loudly,

nodes.title: Grey pants
nodes.snippet: I can't wear them if they are grey. / cheap dusty mourning between their gay attires

nodes.title: Ship hotel and tavern
nodes.snippet: the Ship / The Ship, Buck Mulligan cried. Half twelve.

nodes.title: As others see us
nodes.snippet: As he and others see me.

nodes.title: Caliban
nodes.snippet: The rage of Caliban at not seeing his face in a mirror

nodes.title: Cracked lookingglass
nodes.snippet: a symbol of Irish art. The cracked lookingglass of a servant.

nodes.title: Flesh of a different man
nodes.snippet: linked his arm / freed his arm

nodes.title: Into the army
nodes.snippet: selling jalap to Zulus or some bloody swindle / going in for the army. / up the pole?

nodes.title: Ragging
nodes.snippet: a ragging worse than they gave Clive Kempthorpe. / With slit ribbons of his shirt whipping the air he hops and hobbles round the table, with trousers down at heels, chased by Ades of Magdalen with the tailor's / shears. A scared calf's face gilded with marmalade. I don't want to be debagged! Don't you play the giddy ox with me!

nodes.title: Break the news to her
nodes.snippet: Break the news to her gently

nodes.title: Ideas and sensations
nodes.snippet: I remember only ideas and sensations.

nodes.title: Pulses beating
nodes.snippet: O, it's only Dedalus whose mother is beastly dead. / flush / gaping wounds

nodes.title: Love's bitter mystery
nodes.snippet: And no more turn aside and brood / Upon love's bitter mystery / For Fergus rules the brazen cars.

nodes.title: Cloud cover
nodes.snippet: A cloud began to cover the sun slowly, / warm running sunlight / Warm sunshine merrying over the sea

nodes.title: old Royce sing
nodes.snippet: old Royce sing / in the pantomime of Turko the terrible

nodes.title: Invisibility
nodes.snippet: I am the boy / That can enjoy / Invisibility.

nodes.title: Liliata rutilantium
nodes.snippet: Liliata rutilantium te confessorum turma circumdet: iubilantium te virginum chorus excipiat.

nodes.title: Coronation Day
nodes.snippet: O, won't we have a merry time, / Drinking whisky, beer and wine! / On coronation,

nodes.title: Tower livingroom
nodes.snippet: In the gloomy domed livingroom of the tower Buck Mulligan's gowned form moved briskly about the hearth to and fro, hiding and revealing its yellow glow. Two shafts of soft daylight fell across the flagged floor from the high barbacans / rose from the hammock where it had been sitting, went to the doorway and pulled open the inner doors. / holdfast of the hammock

nodes.title: Crossed
nodes.snippet: In nomine Patris et Filii et Spiritus Sancti. / crossed himself piously

nodes.title: Dundrum press
nodes.snippet: That's folk, he said very earnestly, for your book, Haines. Five lines of text and ten pages of notes about the folk and / the fishgods of Dundrum. Printed by the weird sisters in the year of the big wind. / in the Mabinogion or is it in the Upanishads?

nodes.title: Mary Ann
nodes.snippet: Mary Ann. / For old Mary Ann / She doesn't care a damn.

nodes.title: Breasts
nodes.snippet: rich white / milk, not hers. Old shrunken paps.

nodes.title: Maybe a messenger
nodes.snippet: maybe a messenger. / lowly form of an immortal

nodes.title: Shan Van Vocht
nodes.snippet: Silk of the kine and poor old woman

nodes.title: Shrive and oil
nodes.snippet: shrive and oil for the grave

nodes.title: Speak Irish
nodes.snippet: — Irish, Buck Mulligan said. Is there Gaelic on you? / — I thought it was Irish, she said, by the sound of it. Are you from the west, sir?

nodes.title: Ask nothing more of me
nodes.snippet: Ask nothing more of me, sweet. All I can give you I give. / Heart of my heart, were it more, / More would be laid at your feet.

nodes.title: Do his duty
nodes.snippet: Ireland expects that every man this day will do his duty.

nodes.title: Gulfstream
nodes.snippet: All Ireland is washed by the gulfstream

nodes.title: Haines's chapbook
nodes.snippet: I intend to make a collection of your sayings

nodes.title: Agenbite of inwit
nodes.snippet: They wash and tub and scrub. Agenbite of inwit. Conscience. Yet here's a spot.

nodes.title: Hear him on Hamlet
nodes.snippet: hear him on Hamlet / your idea of Hamlet

nodes.title: Usurper
nodes.snippet: Why don't you play them as I do? / Usurper.

nodes.title: I contradict myself
nodes.snippet: Contradiction. Do I contradict myself? Very well then

nodes.title: Did I not take it up?
nodes.snippet: Stephen picked it up and put it on.

nodes.title: You have eaten all
nodes.snippet: You have eaten all we left, I suppose.

nodes.title: That's not Mulcahy
nodes.snippet: — And going forth he met Butterly.

nodes.title: Ashplant
nodes.snippet: ashplant / Its ferrule followed lightly on the path, squealing at his heels. My familiar,

nodes.title: Down, sir!
nodes.snippet: Down, sir! How dare you, sir!

nodes.title: Martello towers
nodes.snippet: Martello you call it? / Billy Pitt had them built / omphalos.

nodes.snippet: Thomas Aquinas and the fiftyfive reasons

nodes.title: Stole
nodes.snippet: slung his towel stolewise round his neck

nodes.snippet: Japhet in search of a father!

nodes.snippet: The sacred pint alone can unbind the tongue of Dedalus

nodes.title: Consubstantiality
nodes.snippet: The Father and the Son idea. / the consubstantiality of the Son with the Father

nodes.title: My father's a bird
nodes.snippet: My mother's a jew, my father's a bird

nodes.title: No free drinks
nodes.snippet: If anyone thinks that I amn't divine / He'll get no free drinks when I'm making the wine / But have to drink water and wish it were plain

nodes.title: Fortyfoot hole
nodes.snippet: down towards the fortyfoot hole / creek / the middle of the creek

nodes.title: Green stone
nodes.snippet: a smooth silver case in which / twinkled a green stone

nodes.title: Salt bread
nodes.snippet: Now I eat his salt bread.

nodes.title: Two masters
nodes.snippet: I am the servant of two masters, Stephen said, an English and an Italian.

nodes.title: Crazy queen
nodes.snippet: A crazy queen, old and jealous. Kneel down before me.

nodes.title: History is to blame
nodes.snippet: It seems history is to blame.

nodes.title: Creed
nodes.snippet: et unam sanctam catholicam / et apostolicam ecclesiam: the slow growth and change of rite and dogma like his own rare thoughts, a chemistry of stars. Symbol of the apostles

nodes.title: Archangel Michael
nodes.snippet: the vigilant angel of the church militant / Michael's host,

nodes.title: Applause
nodes.snippet: Hear, hear! Prolonged applause. Zut! Nom de Dieu!

nodes.title: German Jews
nodes.snippet: I don't want to see my country fall into the hands of German jews either. That's our national problem, I'm afraid, just now.

nodes.title: High tide
nodes.snippet: the tide comes in about one

nodes.title: Seal's head
nodes.snippet: moved slowly frogwise his green legs in the deep jelly of the water / A sleek brown head, a seal's

nodes.title: Nimbus and niche
nodes.snippet: garland of grey hair / nimbus in a niche

nodes.title: Uebermensch
nodes.snippet: My twelfth rib is gone, he cried. I'm the Uebermensch. / He who stealeth from the poor lendeth to the Lord. Thus spake Zarathustra.

nodes.title: Quarter to
nodes.snippet: Liliata rutilantium. / Turma circumdet. / Iubilantium te virginum.
